2026-05-05.patch_summary: This patch removes the zeromap bitmap from the swapfile code in the Linux kernel. The zeromap bitmap was used to track zero pages, but since zero swap entries are now treated as a separate backend in the virtual swap layer, this bitmap is no longer needed. Removing it saves memory overhead by reducing the number of bits required per swap page. This change does not affect the behavior of the system and only optimizes memory usage.
